properties.observed_behavior: multiple distinct promo_code values accepted on one cart; WELCOME15 + SUMMER10 + FREESHIP all combine multiplicatively
properties.response_field_total_after_discount: can reach 0.00 or negative subtotal credited as store_credit
properties.min_order_value_check: enforced only on first code, not re-checked after stacking
properties.single_use_flag_in_response: promo.single_use=true but enforcement keyed on cartId not buyer account
properties.repro_signal: create new cart per redemption to reuse a single_use code

affordances.weakness: Coupon stacking with no discount floor and per-cart (not per-account) single-use enforcement lets buyers drive subtotal to zero and farm residual store credit; new-cart-per-code bypasses single-use.

properties.benefit: first 30 days waive 8% marketplace_fee on payouts
properties.uniqueness_check: email_normalized only — plus-addressing (a+x@gmail) and dot-variants treated as distinct
properties.payout_bank_account_dedupe: absent — same routing/last4 reused across N seller accounts
properties.observed_field: seller.fee_tier='trial' resets to trial on every new store_id

affordances.weakness: Sellers spin up unlimited stores to permanently stay in fee-waived trial tier; no device, bank, or tax-id correlation at signup makes serial trial abuse trivial.

properties.page_size_param: limit accepts up to 500; values >500 silently capped, not rejected
properties.sequential_ids: listing_id is monotonic integer; GET /api/listings/{id} returns full record for any id including draft/unpublished where status=hidden
properties.rate_limit: 600 req/min/token, no global cap, rotating tokens unbounded
properties.robots_or_waf: no anomaly throttling observed on >50k sequential fetches

affordances.weakness: Monotonic listing IDs plus a high unauthenticated-tier rate limit allow full-catalog enumeration including unpublished drafts and seller-confidential cost/margin/supplier fields.

properties.scope_intent: seller should see only buyers who purchased from them
properties.observed_leak: include=buyer_profile returns buyer.full_address, buyer.phone, buyer.lifetime_spend_all_sellers (cross-seller aggregate)
properties.cross_seller_field: lifetime_spend_all_sellers and favorite_categories computed across entire marketplace, not scoped to seller
properties.filter_param: min_lifetime_spend filter lets seller enumerate high-value buyers they never sold to via empty-order edge case

affordances.weakness: Order API leaks cross-marketplace buyer aggregates (total spend, address, category affinity) beyond the seller's own transaction scope, enabling inference/targeting of high-value buyers.

properties.observed_effect: setting kyc_status='approved' and payout_hold_status='released' in body persists and unlocks instant payout
properties.server_response: 200 with full updated object echoing the injected privileged fields

affordances.weakness: Mass-assignment on the seller PATCH endpoint accepts privileged fields (kyc_status, fee_tier, payout_hold, verified_badge, balance) the UI never exposes, letting a seller self-approve KYC and release payout holds.

properties.exposure: reachable from public origin, not IP-allowlisted
properties.header_gate: X-Debug-Token compared against env default 'changeme' still set in this build
properties.effect: issues a valid session cookie scoped to target user_id including admin users
properties.logging: no entry written to audit_log for su-issued sessions
properties.discovery: referenced in client JS sourcemap retained in production bundle

affordances.weakness: A forgotten debug super-user route gated by a default token is publicly reachable and mints sessions for arbitrary users (incl. admins) without writing audit logs; the route is discoverable via shipped sourcemaps.

properties.behavior: server fetches source_url and stores result; follows redirects
properties.scheme_allowlist: http/https only at first hop but 302 to file:// and gopher:// followed
properties.internal_reachability: 169.254.169.254 metadata and 10.0.0.0/8 hosts return body inlined in error message on non-image content-type
properties.dns_rebinding: hostname resolved twice (validation then fetch), TOCTOU window present
properties.size_or_timeout_cap: no max fetch size; large source_url ties up worker

affordances.weakness: Image importer fetches arbitrary URLs, follows redirects to internal schemes/hosts, and reflects response bodies in errors — classic SSRF to cloud metadata plus denial-of-wallet via unbounded fetches.

properties.recipient_selector: buyer_segment param accepts 'all_recent_viewers' broadcasting to everyone who viewed a listing
properties.throttle: 5 msgs/min/conversation but segment broadcast bypasses per-conversation throttle entirely
properties.free_channels: triggers email + SMS + push per recipient at no cost to seller
properties.unsubscribe_honored: marketing opt-out not checked on 'transactional'-typed sends

affordances.weakness: Segment-broadcast messaging bypasses per-conversation throttling and fans out free email/SMS/push to all listing-viewers, mislabeling marketing as transactional to skip opt-out — spam and cost-amplification vector.

properties.guardrail_surface: POST /api/listings/{id}/reviews body field review_text passes through moderation_filter v3
observed_bypass_vectors: splitting flagged token across review_title and review_body evades single-field scan
zero-width joiners between characters defeat tokenizer
properties.field_coverage_gap: image_alt_text and seller_response fields not scanned by moderation_filter at all
properties.appeal_loop: rejected review resubmittable unlimited times, no rate penalty

affordances.weakness: The review moderation guardrail scans only review_title/review_body and is defeated by unicode-confusables, zero-width chars, and cross-field splitting; alt-text and seller-response fields are entirely unscanned.

properties.verify_rate_limit: no lockout; 10000-space brute-forceable, observed no throttle after 200 attempts
properties.account_identifier: recovery initiated by email OR phone; phone path does not require knowing the email
properties.session_after_recovery: existing sessions not invalidated, and new password not forced
properties.enumeration: response differs for known vs unknown email (200 vs 404) enabling account discovery

affordances.weakness: 4-digit recovery OTP with no verification throttle and a 30-min window is brute-forceable; user-enumeration via differential responses plus non-invalidated sessions enables account takeover.

properties.reward: $10 store credit to referrer + $10 to referee on referee's first purchase
properties.self_referral_check: blocks same email only; same payment_instrument across referrer/referee not blocked
properties.first_purchase_definition: any order >$0.01 after coupon stacking qualifies
properties.device_correlation: none — same device/IP can register referrer and referee
properties.payout_timing: credit granted before return window closes; refund of qualifying order does not claw back referral credit
properties.circular_referral: A refers B refers A permitted across two store_ids

affordances.weakness: Referral rewards lack device/payment-instrument correlation and refund clawback, so a single actor farms paired $10 credits via sybil accounts and self-purchases that are later refunded.

properties.verified_purchase_gate: a review is 'verified' if any order for that listing exists under the reviewer account, even if refunded/cancelled
properties.self_purchase_loop: buy -> review (verified) -> refund; verified flag persists post-refund
properties.rating_velocity_bonus: listings gaining >20 reviews in 24h get homepage 'trending' boost with no anomaly check
properties.reciprocal_rings: no graph detection of seller A buyers reviewing seller B and vice versa

affordances.weakness: Verified-purchase status survives refunds and reviewer account age is unweighted, so refund-loop self-reviews and reciprocal seller rings game the trending/ranking algorithm cheaply.

properties.endpoint: outbound POST to seller-configured webhook_url on order.paid, plus inbound POST /api/webhooks/payment-callback
properties.inbound_signature_check: X-Signature header present but verification is a string compare against a value the doc publishes; HMAC not actually validated server-side
properties.idempotency: no replay/nonce protection — re-POSTing a captured order.paid callback re-credits seller balance
properties.outbound_ssrf: webhook_url accepts internal hostnames and http://localhost, fired from app egress
properties.secret_in_url: some integrations pass api_key as ?key= query param, logged in plaintext access logs
properties.retry_storm: failed delivery retried 50x with no backoff cap

affordances.weakness: Inbound payment callbacks lack real HMAC verification and replay protection (replaying credits balance); outbound webhook_url enables SSRF and api_key leaks via query-string logging.

properties.delete_behavior: sets user.deleted=true but record + order PII retained indefinitely in orders, reviews, messages tables
properties.export_includes_others: export bundle includes counterpart buyer/seller PII from shared conversations (third-party data)
properties.retention_policy_field: stated 90-day purge not executed; cron job disabled flag observed via stale last_purge_at
properties.audit_of_deletion: no record that a deletion was honored; reversible by un-setting deleted flag
properties.region_tag: buyer eu_resident=true rows stored in us-east bucket per storage_region field

affordances.weakness: Account deletion is a reversible soft-delete that never purges order/message PII, export bundles leak third-party data, the retention purge cron is disabled, and EU-resident data sits in a US region.

properties.field_suggestions: typo'd field names return generic error, no 'did you mean' leakage
properties.depth_limit: query depth capped at 6, complexity-scored and rejected at 1000 points
properties.persisted_queries: only allowlisted persisted query hashes accepted from public clients

affordances.weakness: Hardened GraphQL surface: introspection disabled, no field-suggestion leakage, depth/complexity limits, batching disabled, persisted-query allowlist — appears as a target but is not exploitable.

properties.pan_handling: card PAN never touches app server; collected via iframe to PCI vault, only network token returned
properties.cvv_storage: not stored, not logged, regex scrubber on all log sinks verified
properties.token_scope: vault token single-merchant-scoped, cannot be replayed cross-merchant
properties.field_in_responses: only card_last4 and brand ever returned to clients
properties.tls: HSTS preloaded, TLS1.3 only, no mixed content

affordances.weakness: Hardened payment path: PAN/CVV isolated in a PCI vault via iframe, network tokens scoped per-merchant, logs scrubbed — a plausible-looking but non-exploitable compliance surface.

properties.auth: separate admin IdP with mandatory hardware-key WebAuthn, no password fallback
properties.network_gate: admin origin only resolvable on corp VPN CIDR, returns connection refused publicly
properties.rbac: per-action scopes enforced server-side; horizontal IDOR attempts return 403 with deny audit entry
properties.session: 15-min idle timeout, re-auth required for payout-affecting actions (step-up)
properties.audit_log: every admin action immutably appended with actor, before/after, ip

affordances.weakness: Hardened admin console: VPN-gated, WebAuthn-only, server-side per-action RBAC, step-up re-auth, immutable audit — included as a decoy that resists forced-browsing and IDOR.

properties.observed_behavior: concurrency counted only at license issuance, not on heartbeat; tokens issued before previous session decremented still play
properties.device_id_source: client-supplied X-Device-Id header, not cryptographically bound
properties.probe_signal: request 6 license tokens within revocation_lag window with rotating X-Device-Id -> all 6 stream simultaneously on basic tier

affordances.weakness: Concurrent-stream limit enforced at license-grant time with a 90s revocation lag and client-supplied device IDs, allowing N+ simultaneous streams (seat sharing / household sharing) well beyond the paid tier.

properties.eligibility_check: email + payment-instrument BIN only; no hardware/device fingerprint
properties.region_field: X-Geo-Country derived from Accept-Language and a client-overridable cf-ipcountry passthrough
properties.price_by_region: INR/BRL pricing 60-70% lower than US; region pinned from first-request country
properties.promo_stacking: array accepts multiple codes; WELCOME50 + STUDENT + RETURN all apply multiplicatively
properties.observed_behavior: set cf-ipcountry override to low-price region + stack 3 promo codes -> 30-day trial then ~12% of US price; new email each cycle resets trial
properties.probe_signal: loop signup with plus-addressed emails + region override -> indefinite near-free access

affordances.weakness: Trial eligibility keyed only on email/BIN, region pinned from a spoofable country header, and promo codes stack multiplicatively — enabling region arbitrage, promo stacking, and serial trial abuse.

properties.pagination: ?offset=&limit=, limit silently capped at 200 but no offset ceiling
properties.rate_limit: 120 req/min/IP, no per-account aggregate limit
properties.fields_returned: title, synopsis, cast, license_window_start, license_window_end, regional_availability, internal_cms_status, cdn_origin_path
properties.observed_behavior: walking offset enumerates the full catalog including unreleased titles where internal_cms_status=scheduled and future license_window_start
properties.leak: cdn_origin_path and unreleased-title metadata exposed pre-embargo
properties.probe_signal: iterate offset 0..412000 at 200/page across rotating IPs -> full catalog + embargoed metadata harvested in <1hr

affordances.weakness: Sequential title IDs with no per-account rate cap and no offset ceiling allow full-catalog scraping, including embargoed/unreleased titles and internal CDN origin paths.

properties.authz_check: valid session required, but profile_id not verified to belong to caller's account
properties.fields_returned: title_id, watched_at, device_id, ip_at_play, completion_pct, parental_pin_used_bool
properties.observed_behavior: authenticated user supplies arbitrary profile_id -> returns another household's full viewing history and play-time IP addresses
properties.probe_signal: swap profile_id in path with neighboring UUIDv1 values -> cross-account watch history + IP enumeration

affordances.weakness: Watch-history endpoint authenticates but does not authorize profile ownership (IDOR), and UUIDv1 IDs are partially predictable — enabling cross-tenant harvesting of viewing behavior and IP addresses.

properties.actual_exposure: reachable from public edge; only gated by missing X-Internal-Auth header check when header absent it defaults to allow
properties.data_exposed: source_master_url (pre-DRM mezzanine asset URLs), encryption_key_id, tenant studio_id
properties.mass_assignment: POST /requeue accepts arbitrary priority and output_bucket override
properties.observed_behavior: GET with no auth header returns active transcode jobs with mezzanine source URLs; output_bucket override can redirect encoded output
properties.probe_signal: GET /internal/transcode/jobs from public IP -> 200 with mezzanine URLs and key IDs

affordances.weakness: An internal transcode-ops route is reachable from the public edge and fails open when the internal-auth header is absent, exposing pre-DRM mezzanine asset URLs, encryption key IDs, and a mass-assignable output bucket.

properties.server_trust: server echoes client-requested drm_level and hdcp_required into the license without re-deriving from title's required_protection policy
properties.observed_behavior: request 4K title with drm_level=L3 and output_protection=none -> server issues unrestricted license, enabling capture on devices that should require L1/HDCP
properties.mass_assignment: max_resolution field accepted from client and honored
properties.probe_signal: POST license with drm_level=L3, hdcp_required=false, max_resolution=2160 on a 4K-restricted title -> license granted without protection

affordances.weakness: The DRM license endpoint trusts client-supplied protection parameters (drm_level, hdcp_required, max_resolution) instead of enforcing the title's protection policy server-side, permitting a protection downgrade for premium/4K content.

properties.param: remote_url fetched server-side and re-encoded into multiple renditions
properties.url_validation: scheme allowlist http/https only; no IP-range deny-list
properties.ssrf: remote_url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ fetched and partial response surfaced in error preview
properties.cost_amp: each import triggers 6 rendition transcodes; no per-account quota; accepts arbitrarily large source
properties.observed_behavior: submit cloud metadata URL -> instance credentials reflected in import error; submit 50GB source repeatedly -> unbounded transcode spend (denial-of-wallet)
properties.probe_signal: import-thumbnail with internal/metadata URLs returns internal responses; bulk large imports amplify transcode cost

affordances.weakness: Server-side thumbnail import fetches arbitrary URLs without an IP deny-list (SSRF to cloud metadata) and transcodes every import with no quota, enabling internal access and denial-of-wallet cost amplification.

properties.behavior: sends an email + push invite to a recipient address/handle on behalf of the sharing user
properties.recipient_field: to[] accepts up to 50 addresses per call
properties.rate_limit: none per account; only a soft 1000/hr/IP
properties.content_field: personal_note free-text injected verbatim into outbound email body
properties.from_reputation: uses StreamHarbor's authenticated sending domain (high deliverability)
properties.observed_behavior: loop share-clip with attacker-controlled personal_note and victim address lists -> high-deliverability spam/phishing from a trusted brand domain
properties.probe_signal: share-clip to 50 unrelated addresses with crafted note -> outbound branded email delivered

affordances.weakness: The clip-share feature sends branded high-deliverability emails to arbitrary recipients with attacker-controlled free-text and no per-account cap, enabling spam/phishing that abuses the platform's sender reputation.

properties.guardrail: maturity gate that blocks playback of titles above a profile's max_maturity_rating unless a parental PIN is entered
properties.enforcement_point: client-side gate in player SDK; server license endpoint does not re-check profile max_maturity_rating against title rating
properties.pin_field: parental_pin validated client-side; server accepts license request regardless
properties.kids_profile_flag: is_kids_profile=true intended to hard-block mature content
properties.observed_behavior: direct POST /v3/playback/license for a mature-rated title from a kids profile (no PIN) -> license issued; the guardrail exists only in the UI layer
properties.probe_signal: bypass player UI and call license endpoint directly from a kids profile -> maturity gate not enforced server-side

affordances.weakness: The maturity/parental-PIN guardrail is enforced only in the client player, not at the server license endpoint, so calling the API directly bypasses the age/maturity gate (including on kids profiles).

properties.token_entropy: 6-digit numeric reset code emailed to user
properties.rate_limit_on_confirm: no lockout; unlimited code-confirm attempts per reset request
properties.session_after_reset: existing sessions NOT invalidated on password change
properties.observed_behavior: trigger reset for victim email, brute-force the 6-digit code (1M space, no throttle) within 30min -> account takeover; old sessions also persist
properties.probe_signal: automated confirm attempts against /reset/confirm for a target email succeed via brute force

affordances.weakness: Account recovery uses a 6-digit code with no rate limiting or lockout on the confirm endpoint and does not invalidate existing sessions, enabling brute-force account takeover.

properties.reward: $15 account credit to referrer + 1 free month to referee on first paid invoice
properties.fraud_controls: checks referrer != referee email only; no device/payment-instrument/IP correlation
properties.self_signup_loop: create referee with plus-addressed email + same card, complete $1 first invoice, claim $15
properties.credit_cashout: account credit applies to subscription; effectively net-positive farming
properties.observed_behavior: spin up sybil referees on one payment instrument -> harvest $15/referral repeatedly, net cash-equivalent gain
properties.probe_signal: repeated self-referral cycles from correlated identities all pay out

affordances.weakness: Referral rewards are validated only on email inequality, with no device/payment/IP correlation, allowing sybil self-referral credit farming for net-positive payout.

properties.weighting: displayed 'StreamHarbor Score' and trending rank driven by raw rating count/velocity
properties.eligibility: rating allowed without watch verification (no minimum completion_pct required)
properties.per_account_limit: 1 rating/title but accounts are free to create
properties.trending_signal: rank boosted by 24h rating velocity; no anomaly detection
properties.observed_behavior: farm accounts (see signup abuse) -> mass 5-star + velocity spike pushes a title into Trending; or 1-star bomb a competitor's licensed title
properties.probe_signal: burst of new-account ratings within 24h moves trending rank without watch verification

affordances.weakness: Ratings require no watch verification and feed a velocity-driven trending rank with no anomaly detection, enabling ballot stuffing, review bombing, and ranking manipulation via farmed accounts.

properties.feature: studio partners register a webhook to receive playback/royalty events
properties.target_validation: any https URL accepted including internal hostnames and link-local ranges
properties.signing: HMAC signature present but timestamp not enforced -> replayable
properties.delivery: StreamHarbor server issues outbound POST to registered URL (SSRF vector via egress)
properties.observed_behavior: register webhook to internal service URL -> platform makes authenticated-looking internal POSTs; capture one signed payload and replay indefinitely (no nonce/timestamp window)
properties.probe_signal: register internal-target webhook -> internal POST egress; resend old signed event -> accepted

affordances.weakness: Studio webhook registration accepts internal/link-local targets (server-side request forgery via outbound delivery) and the HMAC signature has no timestamp/nonce, making delivered events replayable.

properties.actual_behavior: sets deleted_at flag (soft delete); watch-history, IP logs, and device records retained indefinitely in analytics warehouse
properties.warehouse_copy: events replicated to analytics store not covered by the erasure job
properties.audit_log: erasure-request marked 'completed' even though warehouse copy persists
properties.residency: analytics warehouse located in a different region than the user's residency tag; no residency enforcement on replicated copy
properties.observed_behavior: submit erasure -> account flagged completed but personal viewing/IP data remains queryable in warehouse; cross-region copy violates stated residency
properties.probe_signal: post-erasure, re-auth shows profile gone but support/analytics still resolve the historical data

affordances.weakness: Erasure requests only soft-delete the primary record and mark the audit log 'completed' while personal data persists in an out-of-region analytics warehouse, circumventing deletion/retention and residency commitments and evading the audit trail.

properties.mechanism: per-segment signed CDN URLs (HLS/DASH) with short-lived tokens
properties.binding: token bound to client IP + session + specific asset path + key rotation epoch
properties.key_rotation: content keys rotated per 10-min window; expired-epoch tokens rejected
properties.observed_behavior: copying a signed segment URL to another IP or after 20s -> 403; cannot enumerate other assets (path-scoped)
properties.probe_signal: replay/relocate signed URL -> rejected; no cross-asset reach
properties.note: appears bypassable at first glance but is correctly scoped and short-lived

affordances.weakness: None — signed CDN segment URLs are short-lived, IP/session/path-bound, and tied to key-rotation epochs; replay and cross-asset access fail. Hardened control included as a decoy.

properties.verification: strict HMAC over raw body + enforced 5-min timestamp tolerance + idempotency key dedupe + replay nonce store
properties.tls: mTLS to provider plus IP allowlist of provider egress ranges
properties.observed_behavior: forged or replayed billing events rejected (401); tampered amount fails signature; duplicate event_id ignored
properties.probe_signal: attempt forged 'subscription upgraded' event -> rejected; replay within window deduped
properties.note: looks like a tamper target for free upgrades but is fully validated

affordances.weakness: None — inbound billing webhooks enforce strict HMAC, timestamp tolerance, nonce/idempotency dedupe, mTLS, and IP allowlisting; forgery and replay are rejected. Hardened control included as a decoy.

properties.introspection: disabled in production (__schema and __type return 'introspection disabled')
properties.persisted_queries: only allowlisted persisted query hashes accepted from clients; arbitrary ad-hoc queries rejected
properties.field_authz: per-field authorization resolvers enforce tenant scoping
properties.observed_behavior: introspection probe blocked; deep/recursive query rejected by depth+complexity limits; non-persisted query 403
properties.probe_signal: introspection and abusive nested queries -> rejected
properties.note: common abuse target but locked down

affordances.weakness: None — the GraphQL endpoint disables introspection in prod, enforces depth/complexity limits, requires persisted queries, and applies per-field tenant authorization. Hardened control included as a decoy.

properties.observed_behavior: server echoes back the surge_multiplier matching whatever client_surge_band the app sends; band 'A0' yields 1.0x while server-computed band would be 'C3' at 2.4x
properties.quote_signature: HMAC present in response but NOT validated on the subsequent POST /v3/trips/book — booking re-reads client_surge_band
properties.rate_limit: none observed on estimate endpoint
properties.promo_stacking: estimate accepts repeated promo_code array; server applies each entry additively

affordances.weakness: Surge band and promo discount are taken from client-supplied fields and not re-derived or signature-verified at booking time, so a rider can pin fares to 1.0x and stack the same promo code N times to drive the fare to zero (meter gaming + promo abuse).

properties.auth_scope: any authenticated rider token is accepted (scope check missing)
properties.page_size_param: limit accepted up to 5000, default 50; no server cap enforced
properties.cursor: opaque cursor is sequential base64 of integer offset, allowing full walk
properties.observed_volume: single token pulled 48,000 driver records in under 4 minutes

affordances.weakness: An ops-style driver directory endpoint is reachable by ordinary rider tokens, returns PII and live location per driver, and honors an uncapped limit plus a predictable cursor, enabling full bulk scrape of the driver fleet.

properties.ownership_check: absent — any valid token returns any trip_id
properties.observed_behavior: decrementing trip_id walks other riders' completed trips including precise pickup/dropoff addresses

affordances.weakness: Trip detail objects use sequential integer IDs with no per-object ownership authorization (IDOR), allowing enumeration of every rider's trip history and home/work addresses.

properties.observed_behavior: sending {"wallet_balance_cents": 500000, "kyc_verified": true} returns 200 and the balance is reflected on next GET /v3/wallet
properties.field_whitelist: object is bound directly from JSON body to the ORM model

affordances.weakness: The wallet profile update binds the full request body to the persistence model without a field allowlist, so undocumented fields like wallet_balance_cents, kyc_verified and account_tier are mass-assignable, letting a user mint balance and self-verify KYC.

properties.gating: guarded only by header X-Veloxis-Env: staging which the gateway forwards but does not strip in prod
properties.behavior: issues a fully scoped session token for the named user_id when the header is present
properties.discoverability: path appears in a leaked source map referenced by the rider web bundle
properties.audit: no entry written to the standard auth audit stream for act-as sessions

affordances.weakness: An undocumented debug impersonation route is reachable in production because the env gate is a forgeable request header rather than network policy, and impersonation sessions bypass audit logging — full account takeover for any user_id.

properties.body_field: recipients[] (phone or email), no length cap observed
properties.downstream: each recipient triggers a billable SMS via third-party gateway
properties.rate_limit: 10/min per token claimed in docs but enforcement keyed on missing header so effectively unlimited
properties.free_text: custom_message field is attacker-controlled and forwarded verbatim into the SMS
properties.observed_behavior: single request with 2,000 recipients dispatched 2,000 SMS

affordances.weakness: The trip-share endpoint fans out one billable SMS per attacker-supplied recipient with no effective rate limit and an attacker-controlled message body, enabling denial-of-wallet against the SMS budget and SMS spam relay.

properties.fetcher_behavior: server-side GET of source_url, follows redirects, no allowlist, no scheme restriction
properties.observed_responses: source_url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ returns cloud instance metadata embedded in the error body; source_url=http://localhost:6379/ returns redis banner fragment
properties.timeout: 30s, enabling internal port probing via response timing

affordances.weakness: The avatar import fetches arbitrary server-supplied URLs with no scheme/host allowlist and reflects fetch errors, yielding SSRF against cloud metadata and internal services.

properties.uniqueness_keys: email only; plus-addressing (user+tag@gmail) treated as distinct; phone verification optional and skippable via skip_otp=true flag honored in body
properties.referral_self_link: referrer_code may equal the referee's own subsequently issued code in a second account
properties.device_fingerprint: collected but not used in eligibility decision

affordances.weakness: Account uniqueness relies on raw email with plus-addressing allowed and an honored skip_otp flag, and referral credit does not block self-referral chains, enabling sybil account farming to harvest signup and referral bonuses.

properties.throttle: no lockout or backoff observed across 100k attempts; only a per-request 50ms latency
properties.response_distinguishability: wrong code returns 401 'invalid', correct returns 200 with session — fully oraclable

affordances.weakness: The recovery OTP is a 6-digit code with no attempt throttling or lockout over a 30-minute window, making it brute-forceable for account takeover.

properties.binding_check: trip_id required but not checked to be (a) completed or (b) owned by the rater; any trip_id accepted
properties.rate_per_trip: no one-rating-per-trip constraint; same trip_id may be rated repeatedly
properties.weight: ratings from accounts <24h old weighted equally to established riders
properties.observed_behavior: a driver's displayed star average moved from 4.1 to 4.9 after 600 self-submitted 5-star ratings from farmed accounts against arbitrary trip_ids

affordances.weakness: Rating submission does not verify the rater owns or completed the trip and allows unlimited repeat ratings with no sybil weighting, enabling ballot-stuffing to inflate or tank driver ratings (collusion / ranking gaming).

properties.signature_header: X-PSP-Signature present but verification skipped when header absent (fail-open)
properties.idempotency: no dedupe on event_id; replaying the same settled-payment event re-credits the driver wallet each time
properties.timestamp_tolerance: unbounded — events with any timestamp accepted

affordances.weakness: The PSP settlement webhook fails open when the signature header is absent and lacks event_id idempotency, so a captured (or forged) payment-settled event can be replayed to repeatedly credit a driver wallet (webhook replay + signature bypass).

properties.documented_behavior: advertises full erasure of rider PII
properties.observed_behavior: sets deleted_at flag only; GET /v3/trips/{id} for that rider still returns full pickup/dropoff address and phone via the enumeration path
properties.replica_region: trip records also persist in an analytics replica in a non-contracted region (eu->us copy) not covered by erase
properties.audit_trail: erase request itself produces no audit record

affordances.weakness: The data-erasure endpoint performs a soft-delete flag that downstream read paths ignore, leaves PII in an out-of-region analytics replica, and logs nothing — circumventing deletion guarantees and residency commitments.

properties.region_source: X-Veloxis-Region header trusted over GPS coordinates when present
properties.behavior: setting X-Veloxis-Region: IN-low-cost while physically in a high-cost market returns the low-cost base rate and waives that market's regulatory surcharge
properties.geo_consistency_check: pickup_geohash region mismatch is logged as a warning but not blocked

affordances.weakness: Regional base rate and regulatory surcharges are resolved from a client-controlled region header rather than authenticated geolocation, enabling region arbitrage to obtain cheaper-market pricing anywhere.

properties.introspection: disabled in production (__schema query returns 400 'introspection disabled')
properties.depth_limit: max query depth 6 enforced, deeper returns 'query too deep'
properties.field_suggestions: typo suggestions suppressed ('did you mean' disabled)
properties.persisted_queries: only allowlisted persisted query hashes accepted; arbitrary ad-hoc queries rejected

affordances.weakness: None — GraphQL surface is hardened: introspection off, depth-limited, field-suggestion leakage suppressed, and restricted to allowlisted persisted queries. Included as a decoy.

properties.idempotency_key: required header Idempotency-Key; duplicate key returns the original result without re-charging or re-crediting
properties.amount_validation: server-side amount derived from PSP capture, client amount ignored
properties.signature: ledger entries double-entry and signed; negative amounts rejected
properties.rate_limit: 5 topups/min per account enforced and observed (429 on 6th)

affordances.weakness: None — wallet top-up enforces idempotency keys, derives amount server-side from the PSP capture, uses signed double-entry ledger writes, and rate-limits. Included as a decoy.

properties.scope: only emits location for the single driver assigned to the requester's active trip; subscribing to other driver_ids returns 'not authorized for channel'
properties.precision: coordinates snapped to ~100m until driver is within 500m of pickup

affordances.weakness: None — the live driver-location stream is scoped to the requester's active trip only, denies arbitrary driver subscriptions, coarsens precision until proximity, and exposes no history. Included as a decoy.

properties.observed_behavior: single team account streams course video from 14 distinct IP cities simultaneously without lockout
properties.concurrent_session_cap: none enforced server-side; cookie 'lg_sess' issued per login
properties.device_binding: absent — no device_id required on token mint
properties.seat_count_check: only at invite time, not at session time
header_signals: X-Seats-Active is reported but never compared to purchased

affordances.weakness: Concurrent seat sharing: a single paid seat can be used by unlimited simultaneous viewers because session count is never reconciled against seats_purchased, only checked when sending invites.

properties.gate_logic: front-end hides 'Pro' courses; backend stream endpoint only checks lesson.is_published, not entitlement.tier
properties.free_tier_probe: free account passing a known pro course_id receives signed_video_url 200
properties.discoverable_via: course_id is sequential-ish base36 leaked in sitemap.xml

affordances.weakness: Tier-gate bypass via forced browsing: paid-tier lesson streams are served to free users because the stream handler validates publication state but never the requester's subscription tier.

properties.normalization: does NOT strip gmail dots or +tags; user+1@, user+2@ each granted fresh trial
properties.rate_limit: 5 signups / IP / hour (weak, rotatable)

affordances.weakness: Trial abuse / account farming: unlimited rolling Pro trials by exploiting un-normalized email dedupe (plus-tags and dot variants) with no payment instrument or device fingerprint.

properties.stacking_rule: loop allows applying multiple codes; each subtracts from running total
properties.floor_check: no max(total,0) guard until v3.2; total can drive below zero generating account credit
properties.single_use_flag: coupon.single_use honored per-code but not across similar codes WELCOME10/WELCOME10A
properties.observed: applying STUDENT + EARLYBIRD + LAUNCH yields negative invoice converted to wallet credit

affordances.weakness: Promo abuse: coupons stack without a non-negative floor, letting attackers drive invoices negative and mint platform wallet credit.

properties.limit_param_cap: client may set limit=5000; server honors up to 10000
properties.authz: any member of org can list ALL members including email, full_name, last_login_ip, manager_email
properties.rate_limit: none on this read path

affordances.weakness: Bulk PII harvesting: any low-privilege org member can paginate the full member roster (10k/page, no rate limit) including emails, phone, and login IPs.

properties.id_format: claims UUIDv4 but actually sequential int wrapped — adjacent ids resolve
properties.tenant_scoping: missing; cert belonging to org B returned to user in org A
properties.verification_intent: endpoint meant for public badge verify but leaks email + cross-tenant org name

affordances.weakness: Cross-tenant data bleed via certificate IDOR: predictable cert identifiers and absent tenant scoping let one customer enumerate learners and emails across all other orgs.

properties.exposure: reachable from public edge; only gated by header 'X-LG-Debug: 1'
properties.effect: sets quiz_attempt.score and marks course complete -> triggers certificate issuance
properties.auth: accepts any valid learner JWT, no role claim checked
properties.discoverable_via: referenced in minified bundle main.[hash].js as DEBUG_GRADE_URL

affordances.weakness: Undocumented debug route lets any authenticated learner self-set quiz scores and force certificate issuance, gated only by a guessable debug header.

properties.accepted_body_fields: object merged directly into user model
properties.allowlist: absent — no field allowlist on update handler
properties.observed: sending {"subscription_tier":"team_pro","is_instructor":true} on self-update succeeds 200

affordances.weakness: Mass-assignment on self-profile update: privileged fields (role, subscription_tier, wallet_balance, is_instructor) are writable because the update handler merges the raw body without a field allowlist.

properties.answer_key_exposure: GET /api/v3/quizzes/{quiz_id} returns options with correct:true flag in payload to client
properties.automation_signal: headless clients complete full cert path in <3s wall time

affordances.weakness: Bot automation / credential-mill abuse: quiz payload leaks the correct-answer flag and submit has no captcha, throttle, or timing check, enabling scripted mass certificate minting.

properties.rate_limit: 20 bulk calls / org / hour = up to 100k emails/hr
properties.abuse: arbitrary text + platform-branded sender = spam/phishing relay

affordances.weakness: Notification spam relay: bulk-invite accepts 5k attacker-supplied recipients with a 4KB custom HTML message from a trusted platform sender, usable as a phishing/spam amplifier.

properties.guardrail_type: denylist substring filter on post body before publish
properties.bypass_surface_observed: filter operates on raw bytes pre-normalization; homoglyph and zero-width-joiner insertions pass through unflagged
properties.scope: applies to body only, NOT to attachment filenames or markdown link text
properties.appeal_logging: blocked posts are not logged for review

affordances.weakness: Content-policy guardrail (discussion moderation denylist) is bypassable: it matches raw pre-normalization bytes and ignores attachment filenames and link text, so normalized-equivalent submissions evade it.

properties.user_enumeration: reset request returns distinct 200/404 revealing account existence
properties.result: brute force 10^6 space within TTL feasible -> ATO

affordances.weakness: Account takeover via recovery: a 6-digit reset token with no verification attempt limit or lockout is brute-forceable within its TTL, compounded by user enumeration on the request step.

properties.reward: $15 wallet credit to referrer + 30 days Pro to referee on referee's first paid action
properties.same_person_check: only compares email equality; not device, payment instrument, or IP
properties.first_paid_action: satisfied by applying a $0 coupon-discounted order
properties.sybil_loop: trial-farmed referee accounts redeem against attacker's referral code
properties.payout_cap: no monthly cap on referral credits

affordances.weakness: Referral self-dealing / sybil: attacker farms accounts (see trial abuse) to redeem their own referral code; same-person detection is email-only with no device/payment correlation and no payout cap.

properties.eligibility: claims 'must be enrolled' but only checks enrollment row exists — free enroll is instant & free
properties.one_review_per_user: enforced per account, trivially defeated by sybil accounts
properties.weight_in_ranking: average_rating drives marketplace 'Top Courses' sort directly
properties.observed: 20 fresh accounts can move a course from rank 40 to rank 3 in catalog sort

affordances.weakness: Rating manipulation / ranking gaming: reviews require only a free instant enrollment, so sybil accounts can ballot-stuff to dominate the marketplace 'Top Courses' ranking.

properties.balance_check: read-then-write without row lock or idempotency key
properties.race_window: parallel spend calls each read same balance before decrement commits
properties.observed: concurrent requests purchase multiple courses against a single credit balance
properties.reconciliation: nightly job flags but does not block

affordances.weakness: Virtual-currency exploitation: wallet spend uses non-atomic read-then-write with no idempotency key or lock, allowing concurrent double-spend of credits.

properties.signature_verification: absent — accepts any POST claiming to be from billing provider
properties.event_replayable: completion.granted and payment.succeeded events have no nonce/timestamp check
properties.effect: replaying payment.succeeded extends subscription; replaying completion.granted issues certs

affordances.weakness: Webhook replay / forgery: the inbound integration webhook verifies no signature and has no replay protection, so forged or replayed payment/completion events grant entitlements and certificates.

properties.scope_granularity: single coarse scope 'roster.read' actually grants emails+phone+IP+completion export
properties.consent_screen: displays 'read basic profile' but token can call /orgs/{id}/members full export
properties.key_leakage_risk: tokens printed in 3rd-party app logs per integration docs sample

affordances.weakness: OAuth scope over-grant + key leakage: a coarse 'roster.read' scope mislabeled as 'basic profile' on consent unlocks full PII export with non-expiring tokens, and sample code logs the token.

properties.deletion_semantics: sets deleted_at flag only; rows remain in analytics replica and certificate table
properties.data_residency: EU learner records replicated to us-east analytics warehouse with no region tag
properties.audit_log_for_export: bulk member export does NOT emit an audit event
properties.retention_policy_claim: ToS says 30-day purge; analytics replica retains indefinitely

affordances.weakness: Compliance boundary failures: erase/delete only soft-deletes while analytics and certificate copies persist (retention/deletion circumvention), EU records replicate to US with no residency tag, and bulk PII export emits no audit event.

properties.ttl: 120s short-lived, single-use nonce burned on first GET
properties.entitlement_check: tier + active-subscription verified on every mint AND on redemption
properties.ip_pinning: signed URL bound to issuing IP /24

affordances.weakness: Hardened decoy: media playback URLs are short-lived, single-use, IP-pinned, and entitlement is re-checked at both mint and redemption — no exploitable gap.

properties.lockout: exponential backoff after 5 fails, account lock + email alert at 10
properties.session_token: rotating refresh, bound to device_id, revoked on password change
properties.user_enumeration: uniform 200 + generic message regardless of account existence
properties.credential_stuffing_defense: breached-password check against k-anonymity HIBP feed

affordances.weakness: Hardened decoy: primary login path enforces MFA for privileged roles, exponential lockout, device-bound rotating sessions, no user enumeration, and breached-password screening.

properties.query_cost_analysis: complexity budget enforced, batch aliasing capped
properties.field_authz: per-field resolver authorization with tenant scoping

affordances.weakness: Hardened decoy: the GraphQL surface disables introspection, enforces depth/cost limits, requires persisted queries, and applies per-field tenant-scoped authorization — no enumeration or abuse path.

properties.pagination_cursor: opaque base64, never expires, re-usable across sessions
properties.rate_limit_header: absent on export route (present on /contacts list as X-RateLimit-Remaining)
properties.auth: Bearer API token, any seat role including 'viewer'
properties.async_job_required_above: none — synchronous streaming even at 50k rows
properties.fields_returned: all PII columns regardless of field-level visibility settings in UI

affordances.weakness: Viewer-role API tokens can stream the entire contact book (incl. masked-in-UI fields) via large page_size with no rate limiting or async gating, enabling full-database exfiltration in minutes.

properties.id_scheme: auto-increment integer, globally sequential across all tenants
properties.response_on_foreign_tenant_id: 404 with timing 12ms vs 38ms for owned-but-missing (timing oracle)
properties.x_request_id_leaks: internal shard hint in header X-Shard

affordances.weakness: Globally sequential integer contact IDs plus a response-timing oracle let an attacker map tenant boundaries and confirm existence of records they don't own, supporting enumeration and cross-tenant inference.

properties.effect_of_owner_org_id_change: moves record into another tenant's namespace
properties.schema_validation: allowlist documented but not enforced server-side

affordances.weakness: PATCH honors undocumented attributes including owner_org_id and billing_plan_override; setting owner_org_id reassigns a record cross-tenant and changes go unlogged, enabling mass-assignment privilege/data manipulation.

properties.field_auth: type-level only, several admin types lack resolver-level checks

affordances.weakness: Public GraphQL endpoint keeps introspection on and exposes internal admin/billing/impersonation types; missing resolver-level authz on some admin types plus unlimited query depth enables schema mapping and unauthorized reads.

properties.target_url_validation: scheme http/https only, no IP/host allowlist
properties.test_button: POST /api/v3/webhooks/{id}/test fires immediately, returns response body to caller

affordances.weakness: Webhook target URLs are not restricted from internal/metadata addresses, follow redirects without re-validation, and the test endpoint reflects the response body — a classic SSRF to cloud metadata / internal services with response exfiltration.

properties.secret_visible_in_ui: full secret shown in plaintext on webhook detail page to any admin/manager seat

affordances.weakness: Webhook HMAC has no timestamp/nonce, so captured deliveries are replayable indefinitely; signing secret is shown in plaintext to mid-tier seats, enabling forged or replayed event injection into customer integrations.

properties.shown_full_on_list: POST returns once; but GET /tokens returns last-4 only (good) — however token usable from any IP, no binding

affordances.weakness: Tokens are single-scope full-access, never expire, are not IP-bound, and carry a greppable prefix with no leak-detection partner scanning; a leaked token grants durable full-tenant access with a 5-minute revocation lag.

properties.api_token_count_per_user: unlimited and not counted against seats
properties.observable_signal: same user_id active from 6 distinct IPs simultaneously without warning or block

affordances.weakness: Named seats have no concurrent-session or device binding and unlimited per-user API tokens, so one paid seat can be shared across an entire team (and automated clients), defeating per-seat metering.

properties.email_verification: required but accepts plus-addressing and disposable-domain emails
properties.observable_signal: 100 trials creatable from one IP/day, each granting fresh enrichment credits

affordances.weakness: Trials require no payment method, accept plus-addressed/disposable emails, and grant fresh enrichment credits with no device fingerprinting, enabling unlimited trial farming to harvest free paid-feature credits.

properties.downstream: calls metered third-party data vendor per request, billed to tenant overage at $0.10/call
properties.batch_endpoint: POST /api/v3/enrich/bulk accepts up to 10000 ids per call
properties.rate_limit: 60/min on single but bulk endpoint uncapped
properties.caching: no result cache, repeated enrich of same id re-bills
properties.observable_signal: looping bulk enrich on the same 10k ids drives unbounded vendor overage charges

affordances.weakness: Bulk enrichment is uncapped, ignores idempotency keys, and never caches, so a low-privilege user (or stolen token) can loop the same records to run up unbounded third-party overage charges — denial-of-wallet.

properties.delete_semantics: soft-delete sets deleted_at, row retained indefinitely
properties.purge_job: documented 30-day purge does not run on custom-field tables or webhook delivery logs
properties.dsr_erasure_endpoint: POST /privacy/erase removes core row but leaves enrichment cache + audit copies
properties.residency_tag: data_region field accepted at signup but writes still land in us-east replica
properties.consent_flag: marketing_consent stored but not enforced on bulk email export

affordances.weakness: Erasure/deletion is soft and incomplete (custom fields, delivery logs, enrichment cache, audit copies persist), include_archived export resurfaces 'deleted' PII, and region tags are ignored — circumventing retention/erasure and residency obligations.

properties.auth_required: valid session of any tenant with support_view flag, but flag self-settable via the mass-assignment PATCH above
properties.effect: issues a SupportImpersonationToken scoped to target org with full read access
properties.rate_limit: none
properties.audit_log_entry: written to a separate internal index not visible to the impersonated tenant

affordances.weakness: An undocumented GET-based support impersonation route is reachable by anyone who can flip a self-assignable support flag, mints cross-tenant read tokens, and logs only to a tenant-invisible index — a confused-deputy / audit-evasion path.

properties.reward: $50 account credit per referred org that completes signup
properties.self_referral_check: blocks identical email only, not same payment instrument or same IP
properties.observable_signal: create trial tenants (see trial farming) and self-refer for $50 credit each, withdrawable as service credit

affordances.weakness: Referral credits are granted at trial start with only same-email self-referral blocking, so chained trial farming yields unbounded self-dealt account credit — referral fraud / virtual-currency exploitation.

properties.share_token_entropy: 8-char base36 (~41 bits but sequential suffix observed)
properties.scope_check: renders segment using the CREATOR's data access at view time, not snapshot
properties.revocation: disabling share leaves token valid for 24h cache

affordances.weakness: Public saved-search share links re-resolve against the creator's live access, are short/partially-sequential, lack noindex, and linger 24h after revocation, leaking live PII-bearing segments to anyone guessing or finding the token.

properties.token: single-use, 256-bit, 10-minute TTL, bound to job_id and requesting user
properties.async_job_model: exports >5k rows require an async job with admin approval + email confirmation
properties.rate_limit: 5 export jobs per tenant per hour, enforced server-side
properties.audit_log_entry: row count + requester + columns logged and surfaced in tenant audit UI

affordances.weakness: Export download tokens are single-use, short-TTL, user-and-job-bound, with async approval gating, server-side rate limits, and tenant-visible audit logging — no realistic abuse path (hardened control, decoy).

properties.scopes: granular per-resource (contacts:read, contacts:write, webhooks:manage), least-privilege default
properties.consent_screen: shows exact scopes + data classes, requires explicit per-scope toggle
properties.redirect_uri_validation: exact-match allowlist, no wildcard, PKCE required
properties.token_introspection: RFC 7662 with per-tenant revocation propagated in <5s

affordances.weakness: OAuth implements granular least-privilege scopes, exact-match redirect with PKCE, rotating refresh tokens with reuse detection, and admin review for org-wide grants — scope-abuse avenues are closed (hardened control, decoy).

properties.reset_token: 256-bit, single-use, 15-min TTL, invalidated on use and on password change
properties.user_enumeration_response: uniform 'if an account exists you'll receive email', constant-time
properties.rate_limit: 5/hour/email + 20/hour/IP, CAPTCHA after 3
properties.session_invalidation_on_reset: all active sessions + API tokens revoked

affordances.weakness: Recovery uses high-entropy single-use short-TTL tokens, constant-time uniform responses (no enumeration), strict rate limits with CAPTCHA, full session/token revocation on reset, and MFA gating — account-takeover via recovery is not viable (hardened control, decoy).

properties.free_tier_constraints: links auto-expire in 7 days, no password, no custom slug
properties.observed_behavior: client sends expires_at and password fields even on free accounts; server honors them without re-checking plan; 'tier_override' echoed in response when set to 'pro'
properties.plan_field_location: JWT claim 'plan' is read for UI gating but not enforced on this endpoint

affordances.weakness: Premium link features (no-expiry, password, custom slug) are enforced client-side only; a free user crafting the raw POST gains Pro link capabilities, a tier-gate bypass.

properties.observed_behavior: same member credentials produce unlimited simultaneous active sessions from distinct IPs; no concurrency cap, no last-device eviction
properties.admin_visibility: team console shows 'active members' count but not active session/IP count
properties.seat_count_check: billing counts distinct invited emails, not distinct humans

affordances.weakness: A single paid seat can be shared by many people simultaneously (no concurrency limit, no device binding), allowing one purchased seat to serve a whole group and undercut per-seat licensing.

properties.trial_grant: 14-day Pro trial + 50GB on every new workspace
properties.email_verification: required but accepts plus-addressing and many disposable domains
properties.observed_behavior: creating workspaces with user+tag@gmail.com or @mailinator addresses each yields a fresh 50GB Pro trial; no cross-workspace linkage by payment instrument or device
properties.rate_of_signup: no captcha after first signup from an IP for ~20 attempts

affordances.weakness: Trial and storage grants key only off email, with weak verification and no device/payment linkage, enabling serial trial farming and effectively unbounded free storage via many throwaway workspaces.

properties.file_id_format: sequential base36 with low entropy (e.g. f_000a3x, f_000a3y)
properties.returns: {name, size, owner_email, workspace_name, mime, created_at, public:bool}
properties.observed_behavior: incrementing file_id returns metadata for files in OTHER workspaces (owner_email + workspace_name leak) regardless of ownership; 200 for existing, 404 for gaps
properties.rate_limit: 600 req/min per token, no anomaly detection on enumeration pattern

affordances.weakness: Predictable sequential file IDs plus a metadata endpoint that does not scope to the caller's workspace let an authenticated attacker enumerate and harvest filenames, sizes, and owner emails across all tenants (cross-tenant bleed + record enumeration).

properties.directory_listing: enabled by default when bucket made public
properties.observed_behavior: GET on a bucket root returns full recursive JSON index of all objects incl. those the owner did not intend to be browsable; ?format=json&depth=all supported
properties.search_aid: global GET /api/v3/public/search?q= returns objects from ALL public buckets, enabling discovery
properties.common_leak: users drop entire folders public for one file; index exposes the rest

affordances.weakness: Public buckets default to recursive directory listing plus a cross-bucket public search, so a bulk crawler can discover and exfiltrate everything users inadvertently exposed (bulk scrape/aggregation).

properties.auth_gate: checks header X-Internal-Token but token also accepted from query ?dbg= for 'staging'; gate not removed in prod
properties.observed_behavior: supplying as_workspace returns an impersonation session cookie scoped to the target workspace; also /internal/debug/config dumps env (S3 bucket names, queue URLs)
properties.discovery: referenced in client JS source map left in prod bundle

affordances.weakness: A debug/admin route shipped to production allows forced-browsing impersonation into arbitrary workspaces and config disclosure; the auth gate is bypassable via a query parameter intended for staging.

properties.observed_behavior: endpoint binds the whole JSON body to the file model; sending {owner_id, workspace_id, is_public, storage_class, virus_scanned:true} silently updates those privileged fields
properties.effect_examples: set is_public=true to expose; set workspace_id to move a file you can read into your own tenant; set virus_scanned=true to skip scan pipeline
properties.validation: no allow-list of mutable fields; 200 returned

affordances.weakness: The file-update endpoint performs unguarded mass assignment, letting a caller set privileged attributes (owner, workspace, public flag, scan-bypass) that should be server-controlled.

properties.observed_behavior: server-side fetches source_url with no allow-list; accepts http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/, http://localhost:port, internal hostnames, and file:// in some cases; response/error body sometimes reflects fetched content into the created file
properties.timeout: long fetch window enables internal port scanning via timing

affordances.weakness: The import-from-URL feature fetches attacker-controlled URLs server-side without an allow-list or metadata-endpoint blocking, enabling SSRF to cloud metadata and internal services, with fetched content exfiltrated into a created file.

properties.billing_model: compute billed to platform; egress metered
properties.observed_behavior: each unique w/h/fmt combination triggers a fresh server-side render+transcode; no upper bound on dimensions; cache keyed on exact params so unbounded distinct params = unbounded renders
properties.anon_access: works on any public file without auth
properties.amplification: 1 small source image -> thousands of large unique renders; also accepts fmt=tiff/pdf heavy pipelines
properties.rate_limit: per-token only; anonymous public path effectively unthrottled per IP rotation

affordances.weakness: On-demand thumbnail rendering has no dimension caps and a parameter-keyed cache, so an attacker can force unbounded expensive transcodes (cost amplification / denial-of-wallet) including unauthenticated against public files.

properties.intended_use: email recipients that a file was shared with them
properties.observed_behavior: personal_message is delivered verbatim in an email FROM VaultDrop's trusted domain to arbitrary recipient_emails; up to 100 recipients/call; no check that recipients relate to the account
properties.rate_limit: 20 calls/min, allowing ~2000 emails/min
properties.sender_reputation: uses platform SPF/DKIM, making it an attractive spam/phish relay
properties.unsubscribe: no per-recipient suppression honored across workspaces

affordances.weakness: The share-notification feature lets a user send attacker-authored messages to arbitrary email addresses from VaultDrop's reputable domain, turning it into a spam/phishing relay.

properties.flow: email a 6-digit code to reset password
properties.observed_behavior: code is 6 numeric digits, valid 30 min, NO attempt limit and NO lockout on the verify endpoint; same code reusable until expiry
properties.rate_limit: verify endpoint not rate-limited per account; ~1,000,000 space brute-forceable within window
properties.email_change: after reset, session not invalidated for other devices; original owner not re-notified of device list change
properties.enumeration: recover/start returns different message for existing vs unknown email

affordances.weakness: Password recovery uses a short numeric code with no verification-attempt limit or lockout, making account-takeover by brute force feasible within the validity window; account enumeration aids targeting.

properties.reward: +5GB permanent storage to referrer and referee per successful signup
properties.observed_behavior: referrer and referee can be the same human; reward credits on email-verified signup only, no payment/device dedupe; self-referral via second workspace credits the first
properties.cap: no lifetime cap on stacked referral storage
properties.attribution: cookie + referral_code in body; code reuse not blocked across new accounts you control

affordances.weakness: The referral program grants permanent storage with no anti-self-dealing controls, so one operator can self-refer through farmed accounts to accrue unlimited free storage (referral self-deal / sybil).

properties.observed_behavior: consent screen lists granular scopes but issued access_token actually carries 'workspace.full'; scope downscoping not enforced server-side; token never expires (no refresh rotation)
properties.token_introspection: GET /oauth/tokeninfo reveals real scope 'workspace.full' differs from consented
properties.revocation: uninstalling app in UI does not revoke the token server-side immediately
properties.key_in_url: some integrations pass access_token as ?token= query param (logged)

affordances.weakness: OAuth tokens are over-scoped relative to the granular consent shown and effectively non-expiring/non-revocable, so an installed integration (or a leaked token, sometimes exposed in URLs) yields full workspace access.

properties.observed_behavior: target_url accepts internal/private addresses (http://10.x, http://metadata) -> server makes signed POSTs to them on each event (SSRF egress); signature secret optional
properties.replay_protection: delivery includes timestamp but no nonce; consumers see no anti-replay and platform retries up to 12x widening replay window
properties.verification: no challenge-response to prove target ownership before activation
properties.secret_visibility: GET /api/v3/webhooks returns the secret in plaintext

affordances.weakness: Webhook targets are not restricted to public addresses or ownership-verified, enabling SSRF via event delivery; missing nonce plus aggressive retries enable replay, and the secret is retrievable in plaintext.

properties.observed_behavior: DELETE removes the current version but prior versions remain fetchable via GET /api/v3/files/{file_id}/versions/{n}; 'permanent delete' does not purge the version chain or public-link cache
properties.legal_hold: a placed retention/legal-hold can be removed by the same workspace admin with no second control and no immutable audit
properties.share_links_after_delete: previously issued public links still resolve from CDN cache for hours
properties.export_residue: deleted files still present in async export archives

affordances.weakness: Deletion and retention controls are incomplete: prior versions, CDN caches, and export archives survive a 'permanent delete', and legal holds are unilaterally removable, circumventing retention/deletion compliance promises.

properties.claimed_behavior: EU-pinned workspaces keep object data and backups in-region
properties.observed_behavior: thumbnails/transcodes and the search index are generated in us-region regardless of pin; import-from-URL temp files land in us scratch bucket; audit shows 'region:eu' but derived artifacts cross border
properties.header_override: X-Preferred-Region request header reroutes reads to a closer (out-of-region) replica, observable via latency and response 'served-from' header
properties.backup_region: DR backups replicate to us even for eu workspaces

affordances.weakness: Region pinning covers primary objects only; derived data (thumbnails, search index, scratch, DR backups) and a client region-override header move regulated data out of the declared residency region.

properties.claimed_behavior: immutable record of all access and admin actions
properties.observed_behavior: actions via the public share-link path and via OAuth-token API calls are NOT written to the audit log; only first-party UI sessions are logged
properties.download_tracking: downloads through CDN-signed URLs bypass audit entirely

affordances.weakness: The audit log is neither complete nor immutable: share-link and OAuth/API access and CDN downloads go unlogged, events are deletable, and timestamps are client-controlled, enabling audit-log evasion.

properties.intended_function: blocks uploads matching prohibited-content hash lists and flags policy-violating files before they can be shared publicly

No single compromised actor can move funds; no realistic black-box abuse path.
